---
title: "Foundations & Measurement — Track Map"
---

The toolkit the rest of the course assumes: the mathematics of growth, and how
raw economic activity becomes the numbers we analyze. Click a node to jump
there.

```{mermaid}
graph LR
  PR[Mathematical Preliminaries] --> MG[Measuring GDP]
  MG --> RN[Real vs Nominal GDP]
  RN --> CW[Real GDP with Changing Prices]
  CW --> XC[Comparing GDP Across Countries]
  click PR "../content/Preliminaries.html"
  click MG "../content/measuring_gdp.html"
  click RN "../content/realvsnom.html"
  click CW "../content/two_goods_twoperiods.html"
  click XC "../content/realgdpacross.html"
```

## Suggested path

1. [Mathematical Preliminaries](../content/Preliminaries.qmd) — growth rates, compounding, and the log approximation used throughout.
2. [Measuring GDP: The Expenditure Approach](../content/measuring_gdp.qmd) — output as the sum of consumption, investment, government, and net exports.
3. [Real vs. Nominal GDP](../content/realvsnom.qmd) — separating genuine output changes from price changes, and the deflator that converts between them.
4. [Real GDP with Changing Prices](../content/two_goods_twoperiods.qmd) — base-year versus chain-weighted measures of real output.
5. [Comparing GDP Across Countries](../content/realgdpacross.qmd) — purchasing-power-parity adjustments for cross-country comparison.
